trade_name: NATIONAL BOARD FOR TECHNICAL EDUCATION BLACKSMITHING

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the importance of health, safety, and environment in blacksmithing

performance_criteria.description: Explain health, safety, and environment (HSE) in blacksmithing.
Explain the benefits of maintaining a safe and healthy work environment.
Discuss the consequences of poor safety management in blacksmithing environment

learning_outcomes.description: Identify workplace hazards and implement preventive measures

performance_criteria.description: Explain common workplace hazards (e.g., fire, sharp tools, toxic fumes, heavy lifting, etc).
Explain safety procedures when working with high temperatures and heavy equipment
Demonstrate proper use of personal protective equipment (PPE) (e.g., gloves, goggles, aprons, respirators).
Conduct risk assessment in a blacksmithing environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate safe handling and storage of tools, materials, and waste

performance_criteria.description: Explain the importance of tools and material handling in blacksmithing
Implement safe material handling practices (e.g., lifting techniques, storage of flammable materials, etc).
Apply proper waste disposal methods (e.g., scrap metal recycling, disposal of hazardous materials, etc).

learning_outcomes.description: Respond to workplace accidents and emergencies

performance_criteria.description: Explain the following workplace emergencies 1. Fires 2. Injuries 3. Chemical spills
Demonstrate basic first aid procedures (e.g., treating burns, cuts, fractures, etc).
Follow emergency response protocols (e.g., fire safety drills, evacuation procedures, emergency exit doors, muster points, etc.).

learning_outcomes.description: Apply environmental conservation practices in blacksmithing

performance_criteria.description: Explain the impact of blacksmithing activities on the environment.
Describe methods of reducing environmental pollution (e.g., emission control, waste management, etc.).
Implement eco-friendly practices in blacksmithing (e.g., energy efficiency, responsible sourcing of materials, etc).

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the importance of effective communication in blacksmithing

performance_criteria.description: Explain the importance of communication in the work environment
Explain the consequences of poor communication in the workplace.
Explain different types of workplace communication (verbal, non-verbal, written).

learning_outcomes.description: Apply verbal, non-verbal, and written communication techniques in the workplace

performance_criteria.description: Demonstrate clear and professional verbal (e.g., giving instructions, discussing tasks) and non-verbal communication.
Write simple workplace reports, notes, and instructions.
Use workplace communication tools effectively (e.g., emails, phone calls, fire alarming, messages, etc).

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate active listening and teamwork skills

performance_criteria.description: Explain the importance of active listening in workplace interactions.
Participate in group discussions and team projects.
Show respect to colleagues and superiors through communication.

learning_outcomes.description: Maintain professional workplace behaviour and ethics

performance_criteria.description: Describe acceptable workplace behaviour in a blacksmithing workshop.
Follow workplace ethics and professional conducts and guidelines.
Apply ethical standards in workplace communication.

learning_outcomes.description: Resolve workplace conflicts using effective strategies

performance_criteria.description: Explain common causes of workplace conflicts.
Apply conflict resolution techniques (e.g., negotiation, mediation, collaboration, etc).
Demonstrate appropriate responses to workplace disagreements.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand metal preparation and shaping processes

performance_criteria.description: Explain the importance of proper metal preparation before shaping.
Explain shaping techniques and their applications.
Demonstrate metal preparation in blacksmithing (e.g., filing, grinding etc.)

learning_outcomes.description: Select the appropriate tools and equipment for metal preparation and shaping

performance_criteria.description: Identify the tools and equipment required for metal preparation.
Demonstrate the safe use of shaping tools and equipment.
Select the appropriate tools for specific metal types and shaping tasks.

learning_outcomes.description: Perform metal shaping operations

performance_criteria.description: Explain bending techniques (e.g., hot bending and cold bending).
Demonstrate twisting and coiling metal techniques.
Demonstrate forming techniques using hammering, pressing, and stretching.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate precision in measuring and marking out materials for shaping

performance_criteria.description: Use measurement tools (e.g., callipers, micrometres, measuring tapes) to accurately measure metal.
Mark out shapes and patterns on metal surfaces using appropriate tools.
Apply correct tolerances and allowances precise shaping.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply different blacksmithing processes to achieve desired shapes

performance_criteria.description: Explain the following blacksmithing processes 4. Forging 5. Rolling 6. Casting etc.
Apply blacksmithing processes to achieve the required shape and finish.
Use heat treatment processes (e.g., hardening, annealing etc) to alter metal properties before shaping.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the principles of forging and their applications

performance_criteria.description: Explain the principles of forging, including material properties under heat and stress.
Identify various types of forging operations used in blacksmithing.
Explain the benefits and limitations of different forging techniques.
Explain where each of the forging techniques can be applied

learning_outcomes.description: Select appropriate tools and materials for forging tasks

performance_criteria.description: Select the appropriate tools required for forging
Select the appropriate materials based on forging requirements (e.g., steel, iron, alloys etc.)
Set up heating method for a given material (e.g., gas forge, coal forge, induction heating)

learning_outcomes.description: Perform forging operations

performance_criteria.description: Demonstrate the process of drawing down (lengthening) of metal by hammering.
Perform bending operations to form simple shapes.
Perform upset forging to increase the thickness of the metal at specific points.
Demonstrate punch and drift forging techniques.
Use forging techniques to produce items such as hooks, hinges, or decorative items.

learning_outcomes.description: Use heating methods to achieve desired forging LO (Learning Outcome) Criteria: - temperature

performance_criteria.description: Use various heating methods (e.g., gas forge, coal forge, induction heating) to achieve desired forging Evidence Ref Page Number temperatures.
Control the temperature of metal during the forging process.
Explain the importance of heating control and temperature range in achieving quality forged products.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the principles of tool making and component fabrication

performance_criteria.description: Explain the principles of toolmaking and component fabrication in blacksmithing (e.g., proper material selection, design and planning, heat treatment, tolerancing, surface finishing, quality control, etc.).
Discuss the types of tools and components commonly fabricated in blacksmithing.
Apply the principles of tool making and component fabrication in blacksmithing.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand different types of metals and their alloys used in blacksmithing operation

performance_criteria.description: Explain different types of metals (ferrous and nonferrous) and their alloys (aluminium alloys, bronze, brass, high carbon steel, tungsten, etc.) used in blacksmithing operations
Select appropriate metals for a given blacksmithing operation
Use appropriate metals for a given blacksmithing operation

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate finishing techniques for fabricated components

performance_criteria.description: Explain the importance of finishing in blacksmithing operations
Explain the finishing techniques used in tool making and component fabrication, such as grinding, polishing, sharpening, etc.
Apply appropriate finishing techniques to ensure that tools and components meet the required specifications.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the principles of welding and joining operations in blacksmithing

performance_criteria.description: Explain the different welding and joining operations used in blacksmithing.
Explain different welding and joining techniques
State tools and equipment used for welding and joining in blacksmithing
Describe the advantages and limitations of forge welding, arc welding, gas welding, riveting, brazing, etc, in blacksmithing operations.

learning_outcomes.description: Prepare materials and work pieces for welding and joining operations

performance_criteria.description: Prepare metal surfaces for welding and joining operations.
Set up work pieces before welding or joining operations.
Apply appropriate heat and pressure control techniques for effective welding and joining operations.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate proficiency in various welding and joining operations

performance_criteria.description: Perform forge welding, arc welding, and gas welding operations.
Demonstrate different joining methods such as riveting and brazing.
Assess the strength and quality of welded and joined components.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply finishing techniques to welded and joined components

performance_criteria.description: Explain the types of finishing operations required in blacksmithing.
Use grinding, filing, and polishing techniques to improve welded joints.
Inspect welded and joined components for structural integrity.

learning_outcomes.description: Follow safety guidelines and best practices in welding and joining operations

performance_criteria.description: Identify common hazards and risks associated with welding and joining.
Demonstrate the proper use of PPE and welding safety equipment in compliance with safety regulations.
Apply fire prevention and other safety measures as well as first-aid measures in case of accidents in welding and joining operations.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand Technical Drawings/sketches in Blacksmithing

performance_criteria.description: Explain the importance of technical drawings/sketches in blacksmithing
Identify different types of drawings (e.g., orthographic, isometric, sectional).
Explain the key elements of technical drawings (lines, symbols, dimensions)

learning_outcomes.description: Interpret Symbols and Specifications in Drawings

performance_criteria.description: Interpret standard symbols and specifications used in blacksmithing drawings.
Read allowances, tolerances, and material specifications.
Explain the significance of scale and proportion in metalwork designs.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop Simple Sketches and Technical Drawings

performance_criteria.description: Create freehand sketches of blacksmithing designs.
Use measuring instruments (e.g., callipers, rulers) for accurate drawings.
Produce technical drawings based on given specifications.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply Drawing Interpretation in Blacksmithing Projects

performance_criteria.description: Plan a blacksmithing project based on technical drawings.
Interpret fabrication and assembly instructions from drawings.
Modify or adapt existing designs to meet specific requirements

learning_outcomes.description: Use appropriate instruments in drafting and interpreting drawings.

performance_criteria.description: Identify drawing instruments, such as meter rule, square, compass, protractor, etc, used in carrying out blacksmithing drawing
Carry out measurement using instruments in 5.1 above on a given task
Interpret the measurements in 5.2 into the real product

learning_outcomes.description: Understand material specifications and their relevance to blacksmithing projects.

performance_criteria.description: Explain material specifications.
Explain types of materials used by blacksmith such as carbon steel, alloy steel, stainless steel etc.
Explain the relevance of material specification to the blacksmith project.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the importance of regular maintenance of tools and equipment in blacksmithing

performance_criteria.description: Explain the role of maintenance in improving efficiency and tools longevity.
Identify common issues arising from poor tools maintenance.
Explain the cost implications of improper tools handling and maintenance

learning_outcomes.description: Perform simple Troubleshooting and Minor Repairs on blacksmithing tools and equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify faults in blacksmithing tools and equipment.
Carry out minor repair (e.g., sharpening chisels, fixing loose handles, etc.) on blacksmithing tools and equipment.
Explain when to seek professional repairs of tools and equipment.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop a Maintenance Schedule for Blacksmithing Tools and Equipment

performance_criteria.description: Create a routine maintenance schedule for different tools and equipment.
Maintain a logbook to track tools servicing and repairs.
Review and update maintenance schedules based on tool usage.
